summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the output_path parameter allows writing to arbitrary filesystem locations outside expected directories.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that an attacker can write arbitrary content to any filesystem location the process has permissions, including locations outside the expected output directory.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the target service, submitted a POST /screenshot request with output_path='/tmp/poc_write_test.txt', and verified that the file was created at that exact location, confirming the path traversal vulnerability.
verification_method: The run script executes the exploit and checks if the file /tmp/poc_write_test.txt exists and contains expected content. This distinguishes successful file write from permission errors or output_path parameter being ignored.
evidence_produced_by_run: File /tmp/poc_write_test.txt was created at the attacker-specified absolute path
The file contains screenshot data from the target URL
No validation was applied to prevent writing outside a sandbox directory
The service processed the request successfully despite the malicious output_path

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
